fix: register SIGTERM handler before initial sync cycle

Move signal handler registration to the top of main() so that
SIGTERM sent by Docker during the initial startup sync (which can
run for hours across 300+ TLDs) is correctly intercepted.

Previously the handler was only registered after the blocking
scheduler was created, meaning containers receiving SIGTERM during
the startup cycle would ignore it and keep running until SIGKILL
(30-minute grace period). This caused 3 simultaneous worker
containers during rolling deploys.

commit.message:
fix: resolve 5 critical extraction & verification bugs

BUG-1: Make arithmetic formula regex non-greedy with lookahead to stop
before common delimiters (in/into/then/etc), preventing trailing prose
from being captured as part of the formula.

BUG-2: Add dedicated target-cell extractor for 'in cell X' / 'into cell X'
patterns that runs before range extraction, so the target cell is preserved
even when a range appears inside the formula (e.g. =SUM(D2:D9)).

BUG-3: Pre-strip section header lines and include 'Section N:' in the
split regex so headers don't merge with preceding step text.

BUG-4: Suppress spurious CELL_VALUE tasks when a formula-type task
(FORMULA, LOOKUP_FUNCTION, etc.) already exists for the same line.

BUG-5: Return a warning message in _verify_cell_value when task has no
expected value or formula, instead of a confident pass.

explaining the limitation and suggesting alternatives.

CRITICAL-3: Remove .xls from workbook uploader — legacy BIFF format
crashes openpyxl. Replaced with .xlsm which IS supported.

HIGH-1: Add .xlsm to workbook uploader (covered by CRITICAL-3 fix).

HIGH-2: Add .rtf to instruction uploader — engine supports it but GUI
was silently rejecting it.

HIGH-3: Show st.warning() when both instruction file and pasted text are
provided, informing user that the file takes priority.

HIGH-4: Pasted-text path now shows execution plan — uses
engine.extractor.extract() + engine.plan() to display task breakdown
before executing, matching the file-based path behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(reporter): cover empty-file snippet branch in Human reporter

Add a test that passes a diagnostic with a line number pointing at an
empty source file. The file exists in MockFs but has zero lines, so
`start_idx < total_lines` (0 < 0) is false and the snippet_data
computation returns None — the branch that was previously uncovered.

Also simplify the guard condition from
  `if start_idx < total_lines && start_idx < end_idx`
to
  `if start_idx < total_lines`

The second sub-condition (`start_idx < end_idx`) is always true whenever
the first is true (provable from the arithmetic), making it dead code and
leaving LLVM with an un-coverable branch 3.  Removing it eliminates the
dead branch and brings reporter.rs to 100% branch coverage.

GPU-CPU particles parity: Support factor2 range randomization in gradients (#18283)

## Description

Upgrades the GPU particle gradient texture pipeline so that all
factor-based gradients (`addSizeGradient`, `addAngularSpeedGradient`,
`addVelocityGradient`, `addLimitVelocityGradient`, `addDragGradient`)
support the optional `factor2` parameter. When `factor2` is set, each
particle interpolates between `factor1` and `factor2` using its stable
per-particle `seed`, producing visible per-particle variation, matching
the CPU `ParticleSystem` behavior.

Previously, the GPU system ignored `factor2` entirely and baked only
`factor1` into a single-channel (R) texture, making all particles
uniform at each gradient stop.

## Changes

### Gradient helper consolidation (`baseParticleSystem.ts`)
- Moved `_addFactorGradient` (with sorting) and `_removeFactorGradient`
from both `GPUParticleSystem` and `ThinParticleSystem` into
`BaseParticleSystem` as shared protected methods, eliminating duplicate
logic

### Engine (`gpuParticleSystem.ts`)
- All five `addXxxGradient` methods now accept the optional `factor2`
parameter
- `_createFactorGradientTexture` produces **RG** (2-channel) float
textures instead of R (1-channel), storing `factor1` in the R channel
and `factor2 ?? factor1` in the G channel
- Simplified `_refreshFactorGradient` by removing redundant sort logic
(now handled by the base helper)
- Removed redundant `_releaseBuffers()` call from the old private
`_addFactorGradient`

### Shaders
- **GLSL** (`gpuUpdateParticles.vertex.fx`): All five gradient types
sample `.rg` and interpolate using per-particle `seed` components
- **WGSL** (`gpuUpdateParticles.compute.fx`): Matching changes for the
WebGPU compute path

### Seed component assignments
| Gradient Type | Seed Component |
|---|---|
| Size | `seed.y` |
| Angular Speed | `seed.z` |
| Velocity | `seed.w` |
| Drag | `seed.x` |
| Limit Velocity | `seed.y` |

### Visual Tests
Added 5 new factor2 range visual tests (CPU vs GPU side-by-side):

| Test | Snippet |
|---|---|
| GPU Particles - Change - Size Range | `#HWD2PR#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Speed Range | `#JPPZGY#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Speed Limit Range | `#UQ1RMS#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Angular Speed Range | `#MSX6FE#0` |
| GPU Particles - Change - Drag Range | `#Q1JW04#0` |

## Backward Compatibility

When `factor2` is omitted, both RG channels contain the same value, so
the shader interpolation is a no-op (`factor1 + (factor1 - factor1) *
seed = factor1`). Existing code that only passes `factor1` behaves
identically to before.

## Verification

- ✅ All 5 new factor2 range tests pass on WebGL2 and WebGPU
- ✅ All 12 "GPU Particles - Change" tests pass (no regressions)
- ✅ Lint passes
- ✅ Unit tests pass (2552/2552)

commit.message:
feat: add error accumulation to Table and MutableTable

Replace silent no-ops with error accumulation across both APIs:

Table (immutable):
- Methods record errors via withErrf() instead of silently returning t
- Errors propagate through chains via newTableFrom()
- New methods: Errs(), HasErrs()

MutableTable (mutable):
- All mutation methods now return *MutableTable for chaining
- Methods that returned error (Map, Rename, Set, etc.) now accumulate
  errors via addErrf() instead
- Methods that silently returned on missing columns now record errors
- New methods: Errs(), HasErrs(), ResetErrs()
- Mutable()/Freeze() propagate errors between types

Usage:
  // Immutable chaining with error check
  result := t.Sort("missing", true).Map("col", fn)
  if result.HasErrs() { log.Println(result.Errs()) }

  // Mutable chaining with error check
  m.Sort("name", true).FillForward("region").Map("age", fn)
  if m.HasErrs() { log.Println(m.Errs()) }

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use watch to trigger dashboard fetch when auth resolves in PortfolioView

Root cause: PortfolioView.onMounted fires before App.vue.onMounted's checkAuth()
completes (lazy-loaded components mount before their parent's onMounted). The
auth-gated fetchDashboard() was never called when authentication completed
asynchronously (after the component was already mounted).

Fix: Replace onMounted-only check with a watch({ immediate: true }) on
auth.isAuthenticated. This handles both:
  1. Auth already set when component mounts (loginAs flow)
  2. Auth set asynchronously after mount (checkAuth from App.vue onMounted)

The loading guard prevents double-fetches.

Also adds setAuthUser() helper to mock-api.ts for future use in tests that need
auth without the login form flow race condition.

commit.message:
fix: skip reasoning items when parsing /responses output

The /responses API prepends a {type:"reasoning"} item before the
actual {type:"message"} item when the model uses chain-of-thought
reasoning. Using arr.first() always picked the reasoning item (which
has no content field), yielding empty content despite tokens generated.

Fix: use Iterator::find() to locate the first output item with
type == "message" instead of blindly taking the first element.

Also: switch from resp.json() to resp.text() + serde_json::from_str
for easier debug logging and more reliable parsing.

Add unit tests for both the single-output case and the
reasoning-then-message case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
